prompt: Create a vertical close-medium authentic daily-life photograph of a young Japanese woman in her early 20s sitting sideways on the edge of an unmade bed in a small bedroom on an ordinary morning. Shoot from a slightly elevated three-quarter angle, as if captured candidly, with her posture relaxed and sleepy, leaning forward a little, knees together, one hand resting on her knee and the other loosely holding a dark smartphone near her thigh. Her face is intentionally obscured by a soft rectangular privacy blur, while her naturally straight black hair is loosely gathered and messy from sleep, with stray strands around her cheek and neck. She wears an oversized loose white cotton T-shirt and light gray soft shorts, unstyled and casual. The room feels lived-in: there is exactly 1 large window on the left with bright morning daylight, exactly 1 potted plant on the windowsill, exactly 1 small green bottle near the window, exactly 1 cluttered bedside table with small bottles, jars, and daily items, and an unmade bed with cream pillows and muted sage green rumpled bedding. Use soft directional morning window light from the left, a cool-to-warm transition, visible dust and haze, gentle shadows across the shirt and legs, realistic skin texture on the hands and collarbone, natural fabric wrinkles, shallow depth of field, muted colors, slight grain, documentary realism, no posing, no glamour styling, no makeup, no artificial studio lighting.
